title: Ensure API keys unused for 180 days are detected and  optionally disabled

input_row.title: Ensure API keys unused for 180 days are detected and  optionally disabled
input_row.description: Monitor API key usage in your account and search for API keys that are unused or used infrequently.
input_row.rationale: If an API key is unused for long period in your account, it must be disabled (locked) as a security best practice. All API keys, even those that are used infrequently, must be rotated periodically.
input_row.audit: Console: 1. Log in to IBM Cloud at https://cloud.ibm.com. 2. Go to the Menu icon. Then, select Observability to access the Observability dashboard. 3. Select Activity Tracker from the page navigation menu 4. Check for instances of IBM Cloud Activity Tracker with LogDNA API:N/A
input_row.remediation: You must create an instance of the Activity Tracker with LogDNA service in the Frankfurt region to start tracking IAM events. Use a minimum of a 7-day event search. Generate events and configure a view in Activity Tracker with LogDNA, see https://www.ibm.com/cloud/blog/improve-security-track-api-keys-using-iam-and-logdna
input_row.impact: To reduce the likelihood of accidental exposure or leaking, monitoring for infrequently used API keys can indicate that an API key can be locked and even deleted, if there is no anticipated future usage.
input_row.default_value: By default, API key monitoring with Activity Tracker with LogDNA is not enabled.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control describes the need to monitor API key usage and detect any unused keys, which can be accomplished by checking API metrics.
- The audit process includes accessing the IBM Cloud Activity Tracker and retrieving logs related to API key usage.
- IBM Cloud provides APIs and CLI commands for querying Activity Tracker events related to IAM, indicating this can be automated.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_iam_api_key_usage_monitoring

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Utilize the IBM Cloud CLI command `ibmcloud resource service-instance-keys` to list all API keys associated with the IAM services.
- Use `ibmcloud logging logs` (or equivalent API calls) to query the Activity Tracker for IAM-related activities involving the API keys. This can tell us if the API key has been used or not, and how long it has been since each key was last active.
- Enumerate API keys for the account:
  - List all API keys and their creation dates.
  - Check their last used timestamps from the logs to determine if they're unused for 180 days or longer.
- Pass/Fail criteria:
  - **Pass:** If no API keys are found to be unused for 180 days or more after querying the logs.
  - **Fail:** If any API keys are found that have not been used for over 180 days, indicating a need for remediation to disable them.
- Reference calls:
  - Check activity logs using IBM Cloud APIs specific to user's Activity Tracker checks.
